_comment: Copy this file to config/config.json and fill in YOUR numbers. config.json is git-ignored so your real data never gets committed. Every downstream script reads from here -- this is the single source of truth for identity, accounts, and assumptions. See docs/ARCHITECTURE.md.

household._comment: state_income_tax_rate + local_income_tax_rate are your flat effective rates (set 0 for no-income-tax states). For a progressive state you may OPTIONALLY add a state_brackets array, e.g. "state_brackets": [[0.02, 50000], [0.05, 100000], [0.07, null]] -- it replaces the flat state rate. Fold any state retirement break (SS/pension exclusion) into your rate. See docs/US_RULES.md.
household.name: Your Household

employer_stock._comment: Drives the company-health / RSU module. ticker can be any public company. concentration_pct fields are auto-computed by company_health.py but can be overridden.

healthcare._comment: ACA marketplace premium tax credit, pre-65. Set aca_benchmark_premium_annual to your area's benchmark (second-lowest-cost Silver) plan annual cost to enable; 0 or absent disables ACA modelling. See docs/US_RULES.md.

spending._comment: Optional. 'phases' apply a real-spending multiplier by spouse-A age (the retirement smile: go-go / slow-go / no-go); each entry applies up to until_age (exclusive). 'lumpy' are one-time expenses in today's $ for a given year. Omit the whole block for flat inflation-adjusted spending.

assumptions._comment: taxable_unrealized_gain_pct = embedded-gain share of the taxable pool (drives capital gains on draws); override per-account with accounts.taxable_cost_basis. aca_enhanced_subsidies=false models the current-law 400% FPL cliff returning post-2025; set true for the ARPA/IRA 8.5% cap with no cliff.
